title: Footboard Bench
summary: A slatted end-of-bed bench built around two solid 1x12 sides, with a 2x2 corner frame, a plank base shelf, a plank top with a forward lip, and an optional slatted back.
description:
The two 1x12 side panels double as both the bench's structure and its most visible surface, so their pocket holes go in the back face before anything else is attached — drill them early while the boards are still easy to clamp flat. Build outward from those sides: 2x2 corner posts first, then the corner-to-corner braces that square up the whole frame, then the base shelf boards, and only then the top.

Start the top boards from the back and work forward, keeping the back board flush with the back of the bench — that's what creates the slight overhanging lip on the front edge. The slatted back is genuinely optional; the bench is sturdy without it, so treat it as a place to use up leftover thin stock rather than a required step.

materials.name: Optional back slats, thin scrap wood

steps.title: Trim the sides
steps.body: Nail two 11-1/4" 1x2s to the face of each 1x12, one at the top and one at the bottom end. These become the two sides of the bench.

steps.title: Drill pocket holes in the sides
steps.body: Drill pocket holes in the back of each 1x12 side panel.

steps.title: Attach the corner posts
steps.body: Attach two 17" corner posts to the sides of each 1x12 using screws through the pocket holes, keeping the posts flush with the 1x2 trim.

steps.title: Attach the long frame braces
steps.body: Attach a 32-3/4" 2x2 brace to each corner to create the frame of the bench, using pocket holes on each end of the boards.

steps.title: Add the center brace and trim
steps.body: Attach the center 2x2 brace between the top two long braces. Also attach two 11-1/4" 1x2 trim pieces evenly spaced between the center brace and either end of the bench.

steps.title: Add the base shelf support
steps.body: Attach two 11-1/4" 1x2 pieces to the inside bottom of the 1x12 sides to support the base boards.

steps.title: Attach the base boards
steps.body: Attach the five 32-3/4" 1x3 base boards and one 32-3/4" 1x2 to the base of the bench, with the 1x2 at the front.

steps.title: Attach the top boards
steps.body: Attach the six 37" 1x3 top boards, starting from the back and working to the front. Keep the back board flush with the back of the bench so the front edge overhangs slightly, forming a lip.

steps.title: Add an optional back and finish
steps.body: For an optional back, use leftover thin wood strips (about 1-1/2" x 1/4"), spacing them evenly with a small finish nail to hold the gap as each strip is fastened. Sand and finish the whole bench.
materials: Optional back slats, thin scrap wood

images.alt: A slatted footboard bench with 1x12 sides and a plank top
